_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PANEL
name: Valheim BepInEx (r2modman Profile Import)
author: eggs@goover.dev
description:
A brutal exploration and survival game for 1-10 players, set in a procedurally-generated purgatory
inspired by viking culture incl the Plugin Framework BepInEx.
Custom fork: adds support for importing r2modman / Thunderstore Mod Manager profile export codes.

startup_commands.Default: export DOORSTOP_ENABLED=1; export DOORSTOP_TARGET_ASSEMBLY=./BepInEx/core/BepInEx.Preloader.dll; export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="./doorstop_libs:./linux64:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"; export LD_PRELOAD="libdoorstop_x64.so:$LD_PRELOAD"; export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="./linux64:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"; export SteamAppId=892970; ./valheim_server.x86_64 -nographics -batchmode -name "{{SERVER_NAME}}" -port {{SERVER_PORT}} -world "{{WORLD}}" -password "{{PASSWORD}}" -public {{PUBLIC_SERVER}} -saveinterval {{BACKUP_INTERVAL}} -backups {{BACKUP_COUNT}} -backupshort {{BACKUP_SHORTTIME}} -backuplong {{BACKUP_LONGTIME}} $( [[ {{ENABLE_CROSSPLAY}} -eq 1 ]] && echo " -crossplay ") > >(sed -uE "{{CONSOLE_FILTER}}")

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Valheim Installation Script
#
# Server Files: /mnt/server
# Image to install with is 'debian:buster-slim'
apt -y update
apt -y --no-install-recommends --no-install-suggests install wget jq unzip

## just in case someone removed the defaults.
if [ "${STEAM_USER}" == "" ]; then
    echo -e "steam user is not set.
"
    echo -e "Using anonymous user.
"
    # ------------------------------
    # Steam user defaults
    # ------------------------------
    : "${STEAM_USER:=anonymous}"
    : "${STEAM_PASS:=}"
    : "${STEAM_AUTH:=}"
else
    echo -e "user set to ${STEAM_USER}"
fi

## download and install steamcmd
cd /tmp
mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamcmd
curl -sSL -o steamcmd.tar.gz https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz
tar -xzvf steamcmd.tar.gz -C /mnt/server/steamcmd
cd /mnt/server/steamcmd

# SteamCMD fails otherwise for some reason, even running as root.
# This is changed at the end of the install process anyways.
chown -R root:root /mnt
export HOME=/mnt/server

## install game using steamcmd
./steamcmd.sh +force_install_dir /mnt/server +login ${STEAM_USER} ${STEAM_PASS} ${STEAM_AUTH} +app_update ${SRCDS_APPID} ${EXTRA_FLAGS} +quit

## set up 32 bit libraries
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.steam/sdk32
cp -v linux32/steamclient.so ../.steam/sdk32/steamclient.so

## set up 64 bit libraries
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64
cp -v linux64/steamclient.so ../.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so

echo "-------------------------------------------------------"
echo "installing BepInEx and Selected ModPacks..."
echo "-------------------------------------------------------"

if [ ! -z "$V_MODPACK" ] && [ ! -z "$V_PROFILE_CODE" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: Both ModPack (V_MODPACK) and Profile Code (V_PROFILE_CODE) are set."
  echo "Please only use one mod-installation method at a time. Aborting mod install."
  V_MODPACK=""
  V_PROFILE_CODE=""
fi

# Registers a mod this script just extracted into the same JSON ledger file
# (.valheim-mod-manager.json, inside BepInEx/plugins) that Pelican's Valheim
# Mod Manager plugin reads. Without this, mods installed here are only ever
# detected by the plugin's filesystem scan and shown as "Unknown" (metadata
# only, no Update/Remove/Disable support). Registering them here makes them
# show up fully "Installed" and manageable through the plugin, same as mods
# installed via its own Install button.
#
# Args: $1 destination_directory (e.g. /mnt/server/BepInEx/plugins)
#       $2 wrapper_name          (the "Owner-Name" folder the mod was extracted into)
#       $3 fallback_version      (used only if manifest.json has no version_number)
register_installed_mod() {
  local destination_directory="$1"
  local wrapper_name="$2"
  local fallback_version="${3:-0.0.0}"

  local metadata_file="$destination_directory/.valheim-mod-manager.json"
  if [ ! -f "$metadata_file" ]; then
    echo '{"mods":{}}' > "$metadata_file"
  fi

  local manifest_path="$destination_directory/$wrapper_name/manifest.json"
  local mod_version="$fallback_version"
  local mod_dependencies="[]"
  if [ -f "$manifest_path" ] && jq -e . "$manifest_path" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    mod_version=$(jq -r --arg fallback "$fallback_version" '.version_number // $fallback' "$manifest_path")
    mod_dependencies=$(jq -c '.dependencies // []' "$manifest_path")
  fi

  # Mirrors Chr0mX\ValheimModManager\Support\ModManifestParser::parseFullName():
  # the last "-"-separated segment is the mod name, everything before it is
  # the owner/namespace.
  local namespace="${wrapper_name%-*}"
  local mod_name="${wrapper_name##*-}"
  local mod_key
  mod_key=$(echo "$wrapper_name" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
  local now_iso
  now_iso=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

  local tmp_metadata
  tmp_metadata=$(mktemp)
  if jq --arg key "$mod_key" \
     --arg namespace "$namespace" \
     --arg name "$mod_name" \
     --arg version "$mod_version" \
     --argjson dependencies "$mod_dependencies" \
     --arg now "$now_iso" \
     --arg wrapper "$wrapper_name" \
     '.mods[$key] = {
        namespace: $namespace,
        name: $name,
        version: $version,
        directory: "BepInEx/plugins",
        files: [$wrapper],
        dependencies: $dependencies,
        disabled: false,
        installed_at: $now,
        updated_at: $now
      }' "$metadata_file" > "$tmp_metadata"; then
    mv "$tmp_metadata" "$metadata_file"
  else
    echo "WARNING: Failed to update mod metadata ledger for $wrapper_name"
    rm -f "$tmp_metadata"
  fi
}

if ! api_response=$(curl -sfSL -H "accept: application/json" "$BEPINEX_URL"); then
        fatal "Error: could not retrieve BepInEx release info from Thunderstore.io API"
fi

download_url=$(jq -r  ".latest.download_url" <<< "$api_response" )
version_number=$(jq -r  ".latest.version_number" <<< "$api_response" )

if [ ! -z "$V_MODPACK" ]
then
#Modpack Name dashes to slashes for URL
V_MODPACK=$(echo "$V_MODPACK" | sed 's/-/\//g')

#Extract dependencies from ModPack JSON data
V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCIES=$(curl -sfSL -H "accept: application/json" "https://thunderstore.io/api/experimental/package/${V_MODPACK}" | jq -r '.dependencies[]')
fi

cd /mnt/server
#echo $download_url
wget --content-disposition $download_url
unzip -o denikson-BepInExPack_Valheim-${version_number}.zip
cp -r /mnt/server/BepInExPack_Valheim/* /mnt/server

if [ ! -z "$V_MODPACK" ]
then
#Delete Old Mods
rm -rf /mnt/server/BepInEx/plugins/*

#Download and extract the modpack dlls files
for V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY in $V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCIES; do
  #ignore bepinex
  if [[ "$V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY" == *"denikson-BepInExPack_Valheim"* ]]; then
    continue  # Skip this dependency
  fi
  
  #replace dashes with slashes for url
  V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY_WITH_SLASH=$(echo "$V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY" | sed 's/-/\//g')
  
  #download dependencies
  wget -O "$V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY.zip" "https://thunderstore.io/package/download/$V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY_WITH_SLASH"

  #Set the destination directory and ensure it exists
  destination_directory="/mnt/server/BepInEx/plugins"
  if [ ! -d "$destination_directory" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$destination_directory"
  fi

  #Strip the trailing "-major.minor.patch" to get the "Owner-Name" package folder name,
  #then extract the whole zip (manifest.json, icon.png, dlls, subfolders) into its own
  #folder instead of flattening just the .dlls. Mod managers such as Pelican's Valheim
  #Mod Manager plugin read manifest.json to show the mod's real name/author/version;
  #junking the paths and keeping only the .dlls threw that metadata away.
  wrapper_name=$(echo "$V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY" | sed -E 's/-[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+){0,3}$//')
  mkdir -p "$destination_directory/$wrapper_name"
  unzip -o "$V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY.zip" -d "$destination_directory/$wrapper_name"

  #Register this mod in the plugin's metadata ledger so it shows up as fully
  #"Installed" (not just "Unknown") in Pelican's Valheim Mod Manager.
  mod_version_fallback=$(echo "$V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY" | grep -oE '[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+){0,3}$')
  register_installed_mod "$destination_directory" "$wrapper_name" "${mod_version_fallback:-0.0.0}"

  #Cleanup
  rm $V_MODPACK_DEPENDENCY.zip
done
fi

if [ ! -z "$V_PROFILE_CODE" ]
then
echo "-------------------------------------------------------"
echo "Importing r2modman / Thunderstore Mod Manager profile: $V_PROFILE_CODE"
echo "-------------------------------------------------------"

mkdir -p /tmp/profile

#Fetch with a few retries, mirroring official client behaviour (5 attempts)
profile_fetch_ok=false
for attempt in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  http_status=$(curl -sSL -A "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) ValheimEggInstaller/1.0" -o /tmp/profile/profile.raw -w "%{http_code}" "https://thunderstore.io/api/experimental/legacyprofile/get/${V_PROFILE_CODE}/")
  if [ "$http_status" -ge 200 ] && [ "$http_status" -lt 300 ]; then
    profile_fetch_ok=true
    break
  fi
  echo "DEBUG: attempt $attempt got HTTP $http_status: $(head -c 200 /tmp/profile/profile.raw | tr -d '\n')"
  sleep 1
done

if [ "$profile_fetch_ok" = false ] || [ ! -s /tmp/profile/profile.raw ]; then
  echo "ERROR: Could not fetch profile code '$V_PROFILE_CODE'. It may be invalid or expired. Skipping profile import."
else
  echo "DEBUG: raw response size: $(wc -c < /tmp/profile/profile.raw) bytes"
  echo "DEBUG: raw response head: $(head -c 120 /tmp/profile/profile.raw | tr -d '\n')"
  if head -c 20 /tmp/profile/profile.raw | grep -qi '<!doctype\|<html'; then
    echo "NOTE: Response looks like an HTML page, not profile data (likely a CDN/bot-protection challenge page or error page)."
  elif head -c 1 /tmp/profile/profile.raw | grep -q '{'; then
    echo "NOTE: Response looks like a JSON object, not profile data (likely an API error response)."
  fi

  #Response body is "#r2modman" followed by the base64-encoded zip data - strip the prefix before decoding
  if head -c 9 /tmp/profile/profile.raw | grep -q '^#r2modman'; then
    sed 's/^#r2modman//' /tmp/profile/profile.raw | tr -d '[:space:]' > /tmp/profile/profile.b64
  else
    echo "NOTE: Response did not start with '#r2modman' prefix - treating entire body as raw base64 instead."
    tr -d '[:space:]' < /tmp/profile/profile.raw > /tmp/profile/profile.b64
  fi

  if ! base64 -d /tmp/profile/profile.b64 > /tmp/profile/profile.zip 2>/tmp/profile/b64err.log; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to base64-decode profile data for '$V_PROFILE_CODE'. Skipping profile import."
    echo "DEBUG: base64 error: $(cat /tmp/profile/b64err.log)"
  elif ! unzip -o /tmp/profile/profile.zip -d /tmp/profile > /dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: Downloaded profile data for '$V_PROFILE_CODE' was not a valid zip. Skipping profile import."
  elif [ ! -f /tmp/profile/export.r2x ]; then
    echo "ERROR: export.r2x not found in profile archive. Skipping profile import."
  else
    #Delete Old Mods
    rm -rf /mnt/server/BepInEx/plugins/*
    destination_directory="/mnt/server/BepInEx/plugins"
    mkdir -p "$destination_directory"

    #export.r2x stores each mod as: name / version.{major,minor,patch} / enabled
    #Build "Name-Major.Minor.Patch" dependency strings from the nested version fields
    mapfile -t PROFILE_MODS < <(awk '
      /^[[:space:]]*- name:/ {
        if (name != "") { if (enabled != "false") print name"-"major"."minor"."patch }
        name=$0; sub(/^[[:space:]]*- name:[[:space:]]*/,"",name)
        major="0"; minor="0"; patch="0"; enabled="true"; next
      }
      /^[[:space:]]*major:/ { major=$2; next }
      /^[[:space:]]*minor:/ { minor=$2; next }
      /^[[:space:]]*patch:/ { patch=$2; next }
      /^[[:space:]]*enabled:/ { enabled=$2; next }
      END { if (name != "") { if (enabled != "false") print name"-"major"."minor"."patch } }
    ' /tmp/profile/export.r2x)

    echo "Found ${#PROFILE_MODS[@]} enabled mod(s) in profile."

    for PROFILE_MOD in "${PROFILE_MODS[@]}"; do
      #ignore bepinex, it's already installed above
      if [[ "$PROFILE_MOD" == *"BepInExPack"* ]]; then
        continue
      fi

      #replace dashes with slashes for url
      PROFILE_MOD_WITH_SLASH=$(echo "$PROFILE_MOD" | sed 's/-/\//g')

      echo "Installing mod: $PROFILE_MOD"

      if ! wget -q -U "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) ValheimEggInstaller/1.0" -O "/tmp/profile/mod.zip" "https://thunderstore.io/package/download/$PROFILE_MOD_WITH_SLASH"; then
        echo "WARNING: Failed to download $PROFILE_MOD, skipping."
        continue
      fi

      #Strip the trailing "-major.minor.patch" to get the "Owner-Name" package folder
      #name, then extract the whole zip (manifest.json, icon.png, dlls, subfolders) into
      #its own folder instead of flattening just the .dlls. Mod managers such as
      #Pelican's Valheim Mod Manager plugin read manifest.json to show the mod's real
      #name/author/version; junking the paths and keeping only the .dlls threw that
      #metadata away.
      wrapper_name=$(echo "$PROFILE_MOD" | sed -E 's/-[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+){0,3}$//')
      mkdir -p "$destination_directory/$wrapper_name"
      unzip -o "/tmp/profile/mod.zip" -d "$destination_directory/$wrapper_name"

      #Register this mod in the plugin's metadata ledger so it shows up as
      #fully "Installed" (not just "Unknown") in Pelican's Valheim Mod Manager.
      mod_version_fallback=$(echo "$PROFILE_MOD" | grep -oE '[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+){0,3}$')
      register_installed_mod "$destination_directory" "$wrapper_name" "${mod_version_fallback:-0.0.0}"

      rm -f /tmp/profile/mod.zip
    done

    #Bring across any exported BepInEx config overrides
    if [ -d /tmp/profile/config ]; then
      mkdir -p /mnt/server/BepInEx/config
      cp -r /tmp/profile/config/* /mnt/server/BepInEx/config/
    fi
  fi
fi

#Cleanup
rm -rf /tmp/profile
fi

##cleanup
echo "-------------------------------------------------------"
echo "cleanup files..."
echo "-------------------------------------------------------"
rm -fR BepInExPack_Valheim
rm -fR icon.png
rm -fR denikson-BepInExPack_Valheim-*
rm -fR manifest.json
rm -fR README.m

#rm -fR start_*

echo "-------------------------------------------------------"
echo "Installation completed"
echo "-------------------------------------------------------"

variables.name: Backup Count
variables.description:
Sets how many automatic backups will be kept. The first is the 'short' backup length, and the rest
are the 'long' backup length. When default values are used means one backup that is 2 hours old, and
3 backups that are 12 hours apart. Default: 4.

variables.name: Backup Interval
variables.description: Change how often the world will save in seconds. Default: 1800 (30 minutes).

variables.name: Backup Longtime
variables.description: Sets the interval between the subsequent automatic backups in seconds. Default: 43200 (12 hours).

variables.name: Backup Shorttime
variables.description: Sets the interval between the first automatic backups in seconds. Default: 7200 (2 hours).

variables.name: ModPack
variables.description:
Enter the Dependency String name for a ModPack to automatically be installed
NOTE: If the modpack Updates, you will need to update this variable with the new Dependency String.
This is done to allow Old Versions to be used.
CHANGING THIS REQUIRES A SERVER REINSTALL
Cannot be used together with Profile Code - only set one.

variables.name: Profile Code
variables.description:
r2modman / Thunderstore Mod Manager profile export code (UUID format, e.g. 019f2da9-2a31-0f33-8291-1a960beefb9e).
All mods in the profile, plus their exported BepInEx config, will be installed automatically.
Cannot be used together with ModPack - only set one.
CHANGING THIS REQUIRES A SERVER REINSTALL

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: Name that appears in server browser.

variables.name: World Name
variables.description: Name to load if switching between multiple saved worlds.

variables.name: [System] App ID
variables.description: Valheim steam app id for auto updates.

variables.name: [System] Console Filter
variables.description: Remove unwanted outputs from the console.
